issue: SLM-241 (RSC-A05): Build parameter-, state-, block-, and initialization-matched recursive control constructors
linear_context: Parent campaign SLM-233 (matched recursive-depth quality campaign) is out of scope; this issue only builds reusable control constructors + fairness/accounting substrate.

labels.A: stacked baseline (no z state, unshared blocks)
labels.B: shared recursive V1 (shared blocks, explicit z state)
labels.C: shared y-only recurrence (shared blocks, no distinct z state)
labels.D: recursive no-extra-capacity control (shared blocks, parameter-free z)
labels.E: stacked + matched state capacity (unshared, non-recursive blocks; same-shaped state/state_ctx_proj as B's z_latent/ctx_proj injected once before the blocks run -- mirror image of D)
labels.F: unshared depth-matched tower (block-evaluation-matched against B; no z state, no weight sharing -- MORE parameters than B, see build_arm_f_dual_view for the honest parameter-nearest residual)
labels.G: R=1 shared architecture control (interface-, not behavior-, compatible)

control_arm_table_config.note: Same denoiser-only config as the SLM-138 fixture (d_model=32, max_len=256); tower-level, not whole-TwoTowerModel (context tower is identical across arms and cancels out of any delta, same convention as RSC-A04). Arm F: block-evaluation-matched construction only -- see arm_f_dual_view for the parameter-nearest alternative. Arm E (this iteration): stacked + matched state capacity -- its parameter delta over A equals recursive_zstate_parameter_delta(d_model, max_len) exactly, no dual view needed.

control_arm_table.label: stacked baseline (no z state, unshared blocks)
shared recursive V1 (shared blocks, explicit z state)
control_arm_table.matching_target: none declared -- V1 always adds z_latent+ctx_proj on top of the shared transition blocks (see recursive_zstate_parameter_delta); this arm is not intended to parameter-match A

control_arm_table.label: shared y-only recurrence (shared blocks, no distinct z state)
control_arm_table.matching_target: match A's total parameters exactly when recursive_transition_layers == A's n_layers (no z-state parameter bank of any kind)

control_arm_table.label: recursive no-extra-capacity control (shared blocks, parameter-free z)
control_arm_table.matching_target: match A's total parameters exactly when recursive_transition_layers == A's n_layers (z-state made parameter-free instead of removed)

control_arm_table.label: stacked + matched state capacity (unshared, non-recursive blocks; same-shaped state/state_ctx_proj as B's z_latent/ctx_proj injected once before the blocks run -- mirror image of D)
control_arm_table.matching_target: matches arm B's total parameter DELTA over a same-n_layers arm A exactly -- recursive_zstate_parameter_delta(d_model, max_len) -- via same-shaped state+state_ctx_proj injected once; NOT intended to match A's raw total parameter count (that is C's/D's kind of target, not E's -- E is the mirror image of D)
notes: unshared, non-recursive tower (mirror image of D); parameter_count_delta_vs_baseline=9248 vs recursive_zstate_parameter_delta(d_model=32, max_len=256)=9248 -- matches_formula=True; state/state_ctx_proj consumed once before the transition blocks run, never recurrently re-applied.

control_arm_table.label: unshared depth-matched tower (block-evaluation-matched against B; no z state, no weight sharing -- MORE parameters than B, see build_arm_f_dual_view for the honest parameter-nearest residual)
control_arm_table.matching_target: matches arm B's block_evaluations_per_forward exactly (this row's construction); does NOT match A's or B's parameter count -- see build_arm_f_dual_view for the real measured parameter-nearest alternative construction and its block-evaluation residual instead
notes: block-evaluation-matched against arm B by construction (block_evaluations_per_forward=4); no z-state parameter bank (same as A); MORE parameters than B because nothing is shared -- see build_arm_f_dual_view for the measured parameter-nearest alternative and its block-evaluation residual.

control_arm_table.label: R=1 shared architecture control (interface-, not behavior-, compatible)
control_arm_table.matching_target: none declared -- architecture-change control at R=1, not a parameter-matching arm (same parameter profile as B)
notes: R=1 architecture-change control -- interface-compatible with the stacked baseline, not behaviorally equivalent (SLM-240).

common_tensor_hashes cover only names present with identical shape in every provided arm (tok/pos/kind/layers.*/norm/lm_head when recursive_transition_layers matches the stacked baseline's n_layers); architecture-specific tensors (e.g. z_latent/ctx_proj for the 'full' z_state_mode) are listed under architecture_specific_tensor_names_and_shapes instead.
architecture_specific_seeds are declared/reserved, not literally consumed mid-construction by the current single-pass tower constructors -- see the class docstring.
optimizer_initial_state_hash is the hash of an empty optimizer state (pre-first-step); AdamW allocates exp_avg/exp_avg_sq lazily.

deferred_arms_rationale.H: Stop-gradient recurrence requires detaching y/z between recursive steps inside recursive_outputs and a paired forward-equivalence/gradient-divergence test -- not yet built.

requirement_coverage.1_constructs_and_roundtrips: test_arm_c_d_train_one_step_and_roundtrip_checkpoint (C/D); test_arm_e_denoiser_arch_wired_through_twotower_config_and_roundtrips (E); test_arm_f_denoiser_arch_wired_through_twotower_config_and_roundtrips (F); existing test_twotower_shared_recursive_trains_and_roundtrips covers B; A is exercised throughout the existing suite.
requirement_coverage.2_parameter_counts_match_formula: test_arm_c_d_parameter_count_matches_stacked_baseline; test_arm_e_parameter_count_matches_zstate_delta_formula_exactly; test_control_arm_table_reports_every_built_arm_no_parity_or_winner cross-checks B/G/E against recursive_zstate_parameter_delta and F against B.
requirement_coverage.5_E_consumes_capacity: test_arm_e_consumes_matched_state_and_receives_gradients -- ablation (zeroing state/state_ctx_proj changes the forward output) + gradient flow (state.grad and state_ctx_proj.weight.grad are non-None and nonzero after backward).
requirement_coverage.6_F_block_calls: test_arm_f_block_evaluations_match_arm_b_verified_by_hook_count; test_arm_e_block_evaluations_match_arm_a_verified_by_hook_count (E's analogous requirement, targeted at arm A's block-eval count instead of B's).
requirement_coverage.9_runtime_compat: test_deep_supervision_works_for_arm_c_and_d (recursive_outputs duck-typing); existing runtime-symbol-feature/attention-return/checkpoint-path tests already cover SharedRecursiveDenoiserTower generically (C/D subclass no new code path there).
requirement_coverage.5_F_construction_and_dual_view: test_arm_f_is_unshared_depth_matched_tower_with_no_zstate; test_arm_f_block_evaluations_match_arm_b_verified_by_hook_count (requirement #6, real forward-hook instrumentation, not just len(layers)); test_arm_f_parameter_count_exceeds_arm_b_real_measured; test_build_arm_f_dual_view_reports_honest_residuals; test_arm_f_denoiser_arch_wired_through_twotower_config_and_roundtrips (requirement #1); test_recursive_control_initialization_includes_arm_f_with_disjoint_seed.

tests.net_test_count_change_rsc_a05b: +5 (6 new arm-F tests minus test_deferred_arms_fail_closed_not_silently_built[F], removed since F is no longer in DEFERRED_ARM_IDS)

note: Wiring-only evidence. A/B/C/D/E/F/G are fully implemented (construct through the canonical factory, train one step, round-trip checkpoint, real resource accounting). Only H remains explicitly deferred, not fabricated -- construct_arm_tower raises NotImplementedError for it. Arm F is reported with two honest views (block-evaluation-matched vs parameter-nearest, see arm_f_dual_view) -- never a bare 'matched' claim on both dimensions at once. Arm E's parameter delta over A matches recursive_zstate_parameter_delta exactly (the same formula/target B's delta over A matches), verified against real constructed towers, and its added state/state_ctx_proj is verified (not merely asserted) to be consumed by the forward pass and receive real gradients. No quality or efficiency conclusion is drawn from any number in this document.
recommended_next_iteration: Only H (stop-gradient recurrence) remains. It needs y/z detached between recursive steps inside SharedRecursiveDenoiserTower.recursive_outputs (likely a detach_between_steps constructor flag, following this module's existing z_state_mode-style single-class-plus-flag convention), plus a careful paired test proving identical forward values but divergent cross-step gradient paths vs the non-detached B. This is a genuine test-design problem, not just a constructor change: having now built E, F, C, D, and G, the constructor-and-wiring half of this issue is a well-worn, low-risk path; H's risk is concentrated in proving a gradient-path negative correctly (a residual connection or tied weight elsewhere could let gradient flow through an ostensibly detached path indirectly), so real effort should go into constructing an unambiguous minimal case. SLM-233 itself remains untouched and out of scope.

per_layer_parameter_cost_formula.note: measured from real constructed 1-layer/2-layer DenoiserTower instances, never hard-coded
